ndf.fixing_source_full: Central Bank of the Republic of Turkey benchmark rate
fixing_risk_notes: CBRT sets benchmark rate; strong policy influence over fixings
Offshore NDF market can diverge significantly from onshore spot during stress
Onshore/offshore spread has been large and volatile during interventionist periods

options.delta_label_convention: Delta labels (25DC, 25DP, 10DC, 10DP) refer to the BASE currency, USD. 25DC = 25-delta USD call (TRY put); 25DP = 25-delta USD put (TRY call). 25DC > 25DP reflects topside USD skew — USD calls more expensive, consistent with TRY depreciation risk premium.
options.illiquidity_note: Vol market thins quickly beyond 6M; 1Y is broker-dependent

notes: Very high carry reflects extreme TRY overnight rates
Carry is subject to sudden and severe CBRT policy reversals
TRY carry trades have historically ended in sharp unwind events

market_structure.skew_driver: persistent TRY depreciation trend; political interference risk; current account deficit
market_structure.liquidity_profile: reasonable in spot and 1-3M; can gap sharply in stress

risk_notes: Policy interference: CBRT has a history of politically-influenced rate decisions
Onshore/offshore split: different markets can have meaningfully different rates during crises
Dollarisation: high domestic USD deposit base creates constant structural TRY selling pressure
Reserve adequacy: CBRT net reserves have been negative on net swap basis at times
NDF: offshore market only; no onshore delivery available to foreign counterparties in normal course
